page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.8xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server type does not include bundled local storage, requiring the attachment of external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no integrated graphics processing units on this hardware profile.

Operating on dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, the instance offers predictable compute performance without hyperthreading overhead. This resource density represents a standard general-purpose hardware class, balancing compute and memory resources. The configuration is suitable for workloads such as web serving, application servers, database hosting, and general-purpose enterprise applications that require dedicated CPU allocation and steady-state processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at 3.2 GHz, with a single thread per core. The instance features 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on external storage solutions. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources and a balanced memory-to-core ratio, such as application servers, mid-sized databases, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance featuring thirty-two physical Intel Xeon cores, balanced memory allocation, and twenty gigabits per second baseline network bandwidth.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
